style.name: Style Collection
style.description: Discover apparel and accessories built for everyday comfort, clean design, and an easy premium feel.
style.ctaTitle: Build the rest of the category from the same JSON
style.ctaText: This category is already ready to render cards, filters, and sorting from the catalog file.

coffee.name: Coffee Collection
coffee.description: Coffee gear and daily brewing essentials can be loaded here from the same catalog structure.

wellness.name: Wellness Collection
wellness.description: Wellness items can reuse the same engine, filters, and product-card layout with no extra HTML duplication.

extras.name: Extras Collection
extras.description: Any extra collection can plug into the same JSON-driven category template later.

products.name: Unisex Zip Hoodie
products.shortDescription: Soft cotton-blend zip hoodie with a clean everyday silhouette and easy layering fit.
products.description: A comfortable everyday hoodie designed for casual wear, light layering, and a polished streetwear look. It keeps the visual style from your original template while moving all editable content into a single JSON source.
products.care: Machine wash cold, tumble dry low

items.title: Daily comfort
items.text: Soft feel and relaxed shape for everyday wear.

items.title: Easy layering
items.text: Works over tees and under jackets without feeling bulky.

items.title: Store-ready base
items.text: This layout is already driven by JSON, so edits happen in one place.

sections.title: What can vary from JSON
items: Name, price, SKU, material and care instructions
Available sizes, colors and image galleries
Custom blocks by product type such as features, FAQs or ingredient/spec cards

items.q: Do I need to edit the HTML for content changes?
items.a: No. For text, price, images, sizes, colors and extra blocks, you only edit the JSON.

items.q: Can this later connect to Shopify?
items.a: Yes. The structure is already separated into data and presentation, which makes migration easier.

products.name: Heritage Cap
products.shortDescription: Classic adjustable cap with a clean front panel and easy everyday styling.
products.description: A low-maintenance accessory page that now renders from the same JSON file as the rest of the catalog. Add or replace copy, variants, and cards without touching the structure.

sections.title: Accessory-focused block
paragraphs: This product uses the same detail template, but the content is different because the JSON drives the section data.
For accessories like caps, you can show fit notes, closure type, fabric info, or shipping notes instead of apparel-specific blocks.

products.name: Graphic T-Shirt
products.shortDescription: Premium tee with a relaxed fit and a front graphic meant for effortless daily wear.
products.description: This page shows how another apparel product can share the same base HTML while still keeping its own price, description, variants, and custom blocks.

items.title: Fit
items.text: Relaxed cut built for casual styling.

items.title: Fabric
items.text: Breathable cotton feel for all-day comfort.

items.title: Expansion
items.text: You can later attach design story, print info, or care tips as extra cards.

products.name: Premium Sneakers
products.shortDescription: Clean everyday sneakers with a sporty profile and versatile styling.
products.description: Footwear can use the same global product engine while showing its own sizes, specs, and future feature blocks like sole details or fit recommendations.
products.care: Wipe clean with a damp cloth

products.name: Everyday Backpack
products.shortDescription: Compact daily backpack with a streamlined shape and room for essentials.
products.description: Another example of a product page generated by the same HTML and JSON combination. Good for bags, accessories, or mixed collections.

sections.title: Mixed product types also fit
paragraphs: Bags and accessories do not need a different HTML file anymore.
You can decide their extra blocks in JSON depending on the type of product.

products.name: Statement Sunglasses
products.shortDescription: Lightweight sunglasses with a simple shape designed to finish off casual looks.
products.description: This sample keeps the same product shell and swaps in different details from the JSON. It is a good example for accessories that need fewer variant fields.
products.care: Store in case when not in use

items.q: Can each product type have different sections?
items.a: Yes. A sunglasses product can show lens info, a hoodie can show fabric and fit, and a coffee item can show brew notes or instructions, all from JSON.

items.q: Do category pages need another system?
items.a: No. The same JSON can feed cards, filters, sorting, counters, and links for category pages too.
